channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Can conservative Christian churches win back young women?
item.description: Conservative Christian leaders say winning over young women is a priority. Kathryn Post of Religion News Service explains how groups like Turning Point USA are responding.
item.content:encoded: Conservative Christian leaders say winning over young women is a priority. Kathryn Post of Religion News Service explains how groups like Turning Point USA are responding.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Officials are scrambling to understand the scope of the Ebola outbreak
item.description: Ebola cases are rising in Congo and Uganda. NPR's Jonathan Lambert explains why the outbreak may be even larger than official numbers show.
item.content:encoded: Ebola cases are rising in Congo and Uganda. NPR's Jonathan Lambert explains why the outbreak may be even larger than official numbers show.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: India's frustrated students find a symbol: the cockroach
item.description: Young Indians frustrated by unemployment and exam scandals are rallying behind an unusual symbol: the cockroach. NPR's Diaa Hadid reports from New Delhi.
item.content:encoded: Young Indians frustrated by unemployment and exam scandals are rallying behind an unusual symbol: the cockroach. NPR's Diaa Hadid reports from New Delhi.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: Jobs keep growing despite immigration crackdown
item.description: Construction firms and restaurants are still hiring despite an immigration crackdown. NPR's Scott Horsley explains what the latest jobs report tells us.
item.content:encoded: Construction firms and restaurants are still hiring despite an immigration crackdown. NPR's Scott Horsley explains what the latest jobs report tells us.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Why one historian uses social media to remember D-Day in real time
item.description: At the National World War II Memorial, historian Alex Kershaw has found an unlikely way to keep D-Day alive: live social media posts timed to the events of June 6, 1944.
item.content:encoded: At the National World War II Memorial, historian Alex Kershaw has found an unlikely way to keep D-Day alive: live social media posts timed to the events of June 6, 1944.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: Election in Armenia becomes a test of Russian influence
item.description: Armenia is trying to move closer to Europe and the West, a move that's creating tension with Russia. Journalist Lucy Martirosyan reports from Yerevan ahead of an important election.
item.content:encoded: Armenia is trying to move closer to Europe and the West, a move that's creating tension with Russia. Journalist Lucy Martirosyan reports from Yerevan ahead of an important election.
item.dc:creator: Lucy Martirosyan

item.title: Who will face Karen Bass? LA voters still waiting to find out
item.description: Results are still coming in from the mayoral primary in Los Angeles. LAist reporter Frank Stoltze discusses who may emerge to face Karen Bass in November.
item.content:encoded: Results are still coming in from the mayoral primary in Los Angeles. LAist reporter Frank Stoltze discusses who may emerge to face Karen Bass in November.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Four years after the Dobbs decision, abortion access looks very different
item.description: The Dobbs decision returned abortion policy to the states. Four years later, NPR's Selena Simmons-Duffin examines how that promise has played out in practice.
item.content:encoded: The Dobbs decision returned abortion policy to the states. Four years later, NPR's Selena Simmons-Duffin examines how that promise has played out in practice.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: From Ragtime to Schmigadoon: What to look for at the Tony Awards
item.description: It was a great year for plays, but a so-so year for musicals. NPR's Jeff Lunden points out what to look for at the Tony Awards this year.
item.content:encoded: It was a great year for plays, but a so-so year for musicals. NPR's Jeff Lunden points out what to look for at the Tony Awards this year.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Deported Filipino sailors say they were falsely linked to child sexual abuse material
item.description: NPR has tracked deported Filipino sailors who say they were accused without evidence of possessing child sexual exploitation material. Almost none have been charged or prosecuted.
item.content:encoded: NPR has tracked deported Filipino sailors who say they were accused without evidence of possessing child sexual exploitation material. Almost none have been charged or prosecuted.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán
